message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="marty-cagan" color="blue" summary="Password resolved, delete timeout approach confirmed">
Steve, quick updates:

**Password toggle**: This is now resolved. Consensus reached with Steve Krug and Heydon: progressive enhancement approach. No-JS baseline keeps the confirm field, JS enhancement adds the visibility toggle and removes the confirm field from DOM. US-1 has been updated and committed (`c80c276`). You can design for both states -- the three-field no-JS form and the two-field + toggle JS-enhanced form.

**Delete auto-revert timeout**: Good call on the 5-second timeout as TypeScript progressive enhancement. That's the right layer for it. No-JS stays in confirm state until the user acts. JS adds the convenience. Spec it when we reach Task #9 (delete).

Ready to go.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good -- both open items resolved. Let me acknowledge briefly.

input.content:
Got it. Both resolved.

**Password toggle**: I'll design for the no-JS baseline (three fields: email, password, confirm password). The JS-enhanced version (two fields + show/hide toggle) is a progressive enhancement detail I'll spec when we get to the TypeScript layer. The form layout works either way -- one fewer field just means less vertical height.

**Delete timeout**: Noted for Task #9. Will spec the full interaction including the 5-second auto-revert as TypeScript enhancement.

Nothing else needed from me until registration starts. Ready.
input.summary: Both items acknowledged, ready for registration

routing.summary: Both items acknowledged, ready for registration
